channel.title: John Wyles dot github dot io
channel.description: Recent content on John Wyles dot github dot io

item.title: Setting up Kubernetes and OpenFaaS on a Raspberry Pi cluster using Ubuntu
item.description: NOTE: This is a work in progress and this guide is not complete NOTE: This guide was based on a gist by GitHub user alexellis for setting up Kubernetes on Raspbian as well as a gist getting started with Raspian for each node which I have made some minor additions to in this post. Steps Open the Ubuntu ARM / Raspberry Pi page and find the link to download the latest image.

item.title: Setting up Kubernetes and OpenFaaS on a Raspberry Pi cluster using Hypriot
item.description: This guide uses a Hypriot blog entry as a massive starting point but goes further to show the steps I took in order to get my kubernetes cluster of three Raspberry Pi computers running. This guide addresses much of the troubleshooting done to fill the gaps and/or “bugs” on this blog entry and applying it to my setup. Also credit to Alex Ellis’ Guide for some of the kubernetes troubleshooting.

item.title: Setting up Kubernetes and OpenFaas on a Raspberry Pi cluster using Raspian
item.description: NOTE: This is a work in progress and this guide is not complete NOTE: This guide was based on a gist by GitHub user alexellis for setting up Kubernetes on Raspbian as well as a gist getting started with Raspian for each node which I have made some minor additions to in this post. Assumptions You have more than 1 Raspberry Pi with at least an 8GB SD card for each You want all of your Raspberry Pi’s to have a static IP You desire all of your networking and orchestration over WIFI rather than Ethernet For troubleshooting that you have access to your routers administrative panel to see attached devices and their IP address For further troubleshooting you may require Ethernet access for diagnosis and further setup Steps Clone my Raspberry Pi Kubernetes Cluster repository and change to the raspian directory: 1 2 3 git clone https://github.
